questions.stem: This illustration highlights permanent circular folds of mucosa and submucosa projecting into the small-intestinal lumen. These folds are most abundant in which segment?

explanation.summary: Plicae circulares are most highly concentrated in the jejunum and decrease toward the proximal duodenum and distal ileum.
explanation.whyCorrect: Plicae circulares are most highly concentrated in the jejunum and decrease toward the proximal duodenum and distal ileum.

questions.stem: This gastric region shows deep gastric pits leading to short coiled glands. The surface and pit epithelial cells seen here predominantly secrete which substance?

explanation.summary: The cardia/antrum surface and foveolar (pit) cells are mucus-secreting, protecting the epithelium from acid.
explanation.whyCorrect: The cardia/antrum surface and foveolar (pit) cells are mucus-secreting, protecting the epithelium from acid.

questions.stem: In this body/fundus gastric mucosa, the large eosinophilic cells (circled) produce hydrochloric acid. Which additional essential substance do these same cells secrete?

explanation.summary: Parietal cells of the gastric body/fundus secrete both HCl and intrinsic factor, the latter required for vitamin B12 absorption in the ileum.
explanation.whyCorrect: Parietal cells of the gastric body/fundus secrete both HCl and intrinsic factor, the latter required for vitamin B12 absorption in the ileum.

questions.stem: This diagram of small-intestinal mucosa shows absorptive columnar cells whose apical membranes bear dense projections that further amplify surface area. What is this apical specialization called?

explanation.summary: Enterocytes covering the villi have apical microvilli forming the brush border, dramatically increasing absorptive surface area.
explanation.whyCorrect: Enterocytes covering the villi have apical microvilli forming the brush border, dramatically increasing absorptive surface area.

questions.stem: The submucosal glands seen in this section secrete bicarbonate-rich mucus that neutralizes acidic chyme. Their presence is specific to which segment of the small intestine?

explanation.summary: Brunner glands reside in the submucosa and are unique to the duodenum, neutralizing gastric acid entering from the stomach.
explanation.whyCorrect: Brunner glands reside in the submucosa and are unique to the duodenum, neutralizing gastric acid entering from the stomach.

questions.stem: These crypt-base cells contain bright eosinophilic apical granules and become more numerous toward the ileum. Which antimicrobial products do they secrete?

explanation.summary: Paneth cells sit at the crypt base and release antimicrobial defensins, lysozyme, and phospholipase A2, protecting stem cells.
explanation.whyCorrect: Paneth cells sit at the crypt base and release antimicrobial defensins, lysozyme, and phospholipase A2, protecting stem cells.

questions.stem: This section shows dense lymphoid aggregates spanning the mucosa and submucosa of the small intestine. These organized nodules are most abundant in which segment?

explanation.summary: Peyer patches are aggregated lymphoid follicles concentrated in the ileum, providing mucosal immune surveillance.
explanation.whyCorrect: Peyer patches are aggregated lymphoid follicles concentrated in the ileum, providing mucosal immune surveillance.

questions.stem: This large-intestinal mucosa shows straight crypts and no villi. The numerous pale, round cells packed along the crypts predominantly secrete what?

explanation.summary: The colon has abundant goblet cells that secrete mucus to lubricate compacting feces; it lacks villi.
explanation.whyCorrect: The colon has abundant goblet cells that secrete mucus to lubricate compacting feces; it lacks villi.

questions.stem: This structure resembles colon (straight crypts, no villi) but is distinguished by abundant lymphoid tissue that tends to obliterate the muscularis mucosae. What is it?

explanation.summary: The appendix mimics colonic mucosa but has prominent lymphoid nodules extending into the submucosa and effacing the muscularis mucosae.
explanation.whyCorrect: The appendix mimics colonic mucosa but has prominent lymphoid nodules extending into the submucosa and effacing the muscularis mucosae.

questions.stem: At the junction shown here, the epithelium transitions from simple columnar to stratified squamous. This squamous lining is advantageous because it best withstands what?

explanation.summary: Stratified squamous epithelium at the anorectal junction resists the forceful, abrasive luminal functions of defecation.
explanation.whyCorrect: Stratified squamous epithelium at the anorectal junction resists the forceful, abrasive luminal functions of defecation.

questions.stem: This histologic field shows an abrupt epithelial transition (the Z line). Chronic acid exposure here can drive metaplasia of the squamous epithelium, which increases risk of what malignancy?

explanation.summary: At the gastroesophageal junction, reflux-induced replacement of squamous epithelium by columnar epithelium with goblet cells (Barrett esophagus) predisposes to esophageal adenocarcinoma.
explanation.whyCorrect: At the gastroesophageal junction, reflux-induced replacement of squamous epithelium by columnar epithelium with goblet cells (Barrett esophagus) predisposes to esophageal adenocarcinoma.
